heading: PDP regions; submission of bids; plan approval
label: 1395w–111 PDP regions; submission of bids; plan approval
notes: References in Text, referred to in subsec. (b)(2)(E)(ii)(I), was in the original “section 1869D–15(e)(2)(B)(iii)”, and was translated as reading “section 1860D–15(e)(2)(B)(iii)”, meaning 1860D–15(e)(2)(B)(iii) of the Social Security Act, to reflect the probable intent of Congress, because the Social Security Act does not contain a section 1869D–15 and provides for an application of a higher percentage for years 2006 and 2007.Section 1395w–115(e)(2)(B)(iii) of this titlesection 1395w–115(e)(2)(B)(iii) of this title

L. 108–173, title I, § 107(a)117 Stat. 2169Dec. 8, 2003“(1).—In generalThe Secretary [of Health and Human Services] shall conduct a study that examines variations in per capita spending for covered part D drugs under part D of title XVIII of the Social Security Act [ et seq.] among PDP regions and, with respect to such spending, the amount of such variation that is attributable to—42 U.S.C. 1395w–101“(A)price variations (described in section 1860D–15(c)(2) of such Act []); and42 U.S.C. 1395w–115(c)(2)“(B)differences in per capita utilization that is not taken into account in the health status risk adjustment provided under section 1860D–15(c)(1) of such Act [].42 U.S.C. 1395w–115(c)(1)“(2).—Report and recommendationsNot later than , the Secretary shall submit to Congress a report on the study conducted under paragraph (1). Such report shall include—January 1, 2009“(A)information regarding the extent of geographic variation described in paragraph (1)(B);“(B)an analysis of the impact on direct subsidies under section 1860D–15(a)(1) of the Social Security Act [] in different PDP regions if such subsidies were adjusted to take into account the variation described in subparagraph (A); and42 U.S.C. 1395w–115(a)(1)“(C)recommendations regarding the appropriateness of applying an additional geographic adjustment factor under section 1860D–15(c)(2) [] that reflects some or all of the variation described in subparagraph (A).”42 U.S.C. 1395w–115(c)(2)

children.heading: Establishment of PDP regions; service areas
children.label: a Establishment of PDP regions; service areas

children.heading: Coverage of entire PDP region
children.label: 1 Coverage of entire PDP region
children.text: The service area for a prescription drug plan shall consist of an entire PDP region established under paragraph (2).

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary shall establish, and may revise, PDP regions in a manner that is consistent with the requirements for the establishment and revision of MA regions under subparagraphs (B) and (C) of .section 1395w–27a(a)(2) of this title

children.heading: Relation to MA regions
children.label: B Relation to MA regions
children.text: To the extent practicable, PDP regions shall be the same as MA regions under . The Secretary may establish PDP regions which are not the same as MA regions if the Secretary determines that the establishment of different regions under this part would improve access to benefits under this part.section 1395w–27a(a)(2) of this title

children.heading: Authority for territories
children.label: C Authority for territories
children.text: The Secretary shall establish, and may revise, PDP regions for areas in States that are not within the 50 States or the District of Columbia.

children.heading: National plan
children.label: 3 National plan
children.text: Nothing in this subsection shall be construed as preventing a prescription drug plan from being offered in more than one PDP region (including all PDP regions).

children.heading: Submission of bids, premiums, and related information
children.label: b Submission of bids, premiums, and related information

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: A PDP sponsor shall submit to the Secretary information described in paragraph (2) with respect to each prescription drug plan it offers. Such information shall be submitted at the same time and in a similar manner to the manner in which information described in paragraph (6) of is submitted by an MA organization under paragraph (1) of such section.section 1395w–24(a) of this title

children.heading: Coverage provided
children.label: A Coverage provided
children.text: The prescription drug coverage provided under the plan, including the deductible and other cost-sharing.

children.heading: Actuarial value
children.label: B Actuarial value
children.text: The actuarial value of the qualified prescription drug coverage in the region for a part D eligible individual with a national average risk profile for the factors described in (as specified by the Secretary).section 1395w–115(c)(1)(A) of this title

children.label: i
children.text: the basis for the actuarial value described in subparagraph (B) assumed in such bid;

children.label: ii
children.text: the portion of such bid attributable to basic prescription drug coverage and, if applicable, the portion of such bid attributable to supplemental benefits;

children.label: iii
children.text: assumptions regarding the reinsurance subsidy payments provided under subtracted from the actuarial value to produce such bid; andsection 1395w–115(b) of this title

children.label: iv
children.text: administrative expenses assumed in the bid.

children.heading: Service area
children.label: D Service area
children.text: The service area for the plan.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Whether the PDP sponsor requires a modification of risk level under clause (ii) and, if so, the extent of such modification. Any such modification shall apply with respect to all prescription drug plans offered by a PDP sponsor in a PDP region. This subparagraph shall not apply to an MA–PD plan.

children.heading: Increase in Federal percentage assumed in initial risk corridor
children.label: I Increase in Federal percentage assumed in initial risk corridor
children.text: An equal percentage point increase in the percents applied under subparagraphs (B)(i), (B)(ii)(I), (C)(i), and (C)(ii)(I) of . In no case shall the application of previous sentence prevent the application of a higher percentage under section 1395w–115(e)(2)(B)(iii) of this title.section 1395w–115(e)(2) of this title1See References in Text note below.1

children.heading: Increase in Federal percentage assumed in second risk corridor
children.label: II Increase in Federal percentage assumed in second risk corridor
children.text: An equal percentage point increase in the percents applied under subparagraphs (B)(ii)(II) and (C)(ii)(II) of .section 1395w–115(e)(2) of this title

children.heading: Decrease in size of risk corridors
children.label: III Decrease in size of risk corridors
children.text: A decrease in the threshold risk percentages specified in .section 1395w–115(e)(3)(C) of this title

children.heading: Additional information
children.label: F Additional information
children.text: Such other information as the Secretary may require to carry out this part.

children.heading: Paperwork reduction for offering of prescription drug plans nationally or in multi-region areas
children.label: 3 Paperwork reduction for offering of prescription drug plans nationally or in multi-region areas
children.text: The Secretary shall establish requirements for information submission under this subsection in a manner that promotes the offering of such plans in more than one PDP region (including all regions) through the filing of consolidated information.

children.label: A
children.text: an actuarial valuation of standard prescription drug coverage under ;section 1395w–102(b) of this title

children.label: B
children.text: actuarial valuations relating to alternative prescription drug coverage under ;section 1395w–102(c)(1) of this title

children.label: C
children.text: an actuarial valuation of the reinsurance subsidy payments under ;section 1395w–115(b) of this title

children.label: D
children.text: the use of generally accepted actuarial principles and methodologies; and

children.label: E
children.text: applying the same methodology for determinations of actuarial valuations under subparagraphs (A) and (B).

children.heading: Accounting for drug utilization
children.label: 2 Accounting for drug utilization
children.text: Such processes and methods for determining actuarial valuation shall take into account the effect that providing alternative prescription drug coverage (rather than standard prescription drug coverage) has on drug utilization.

children.heading: Plan responsibilities
children.label: A Plan responsibilities
children.text: PDP sponsors and MA organizations are responsible for the preparation and submission of actuarial valuations required under this part for prescription drug plans and MA–PD plans they offer.

children.heading: Use of outside actuaries
children.label: B Use of outside actuaries
children.text: Under the processes and methods established under paragraph (1), PDP sponsors offering prescription drug plans and MA organizations offering MA–PD plans may use actuarial opinions certified by independent, qualified actuaries to establish actuarial values.

children.heading: Review of information and negotiation
children.label: d Review of information and negotiation

children.heading: Review of information
children.label: 1 Review of information
children.text: The Secretary shall review the information filed under subsection (b) for the purpose of conducting negotiations under paragraph (2).

children.label: A
children.text: has the authority to negotiate the terms and conditions of the proposed bid submitted and other terms and conditions of a proposed plan; and

children.label: B
children.text: has authority similar to the authority of the Director of the Office of Personnel Management with respect to health benefits plans under chapter 89 of title 5.

children.heading: Rejection of bids
children.label: 3 Rejection of bids
children.text: Paragraph (5)(C) of shall apply with respect to bids submitted by a PDP sponsor under subsection (b) in the same manner as such paragraph applies to bids submitted by an MA organization under such .section 1395w–24(a) of this titlesection 1395w–24(a) of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: After review and negotiation under subsection (d), the Secretary shall approve or disapprove the prescription drug plan.

children.heading: Compliance with requirements
children.label: A Compliance with requirements
children.text: The plan and the PDP sponsor offering the plan comply with the requirements under this part, including the provision of qualified prescription drug coverage.

children.heading: Actuarial determinations
children.label: B Actuarial determinations
children.text: The Secretary determines that the plan and PDP sponsor meet the requirements under this part relating to actuarial determinations, including such requirements under .section 1395w–102(c) of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The Secretary determines that the portion of the bid submitted under subsection (b) that is attributable to basic prescription drug coverage is supported by the actuarial bases provided under such subsection and reasonably and equitably reflects the revenue requirements (as used for purposes of ) for benefits provided under that plan, less the sum (determined on a monthly per capita basis) of the actuarial value of the reinsurance payments under .section 300e–1(8)(C) of this titlesection 1395w–115(b) of this title

children.heading: Supplemental coverage
children.label: ii Supplemental coverage
children.text: The Secretary determines that the portion of the bid submitted under subsection (b) that is attributable to supplemental prescription drug coverage pursuant to is supported by the actuarial bases provided under such subsection and reasonably and equitably reflects the revenue requirements (as used for purposes of ) for such coverage under the plan.section 1395w–102(a)(2) of this titlesection 300e–1(8)(C) of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The Secretary does not find that the design of the plan and its benefits (including any formulary and tiered formulary structure) are likely to substantially discourage enrollment by certain part D eligible individuals under the plan.

children.heading: Use of categories and classes in formularies
children.label: ii Use of categories and classes in formularies
children.text: The Secretary may not find that the design of categories and classes within a formulary violates clause (i) if such categories and classes are consistent with guidelines (if any) for such categories and classes established by the United States Pharmacopeia.

children.heading: Application of limited risk plans
children.label: f Application of limited risk plans

children.heading: Conditions for approval of limited risk plans
children.label: 1 Conditions for approval of limited risk plans
children.text: The Secretary may only approve a limited risk plan (as defined in paragraph (4)(A)) for a PDP region if the access requirements under would not be met for the region but for the approval of such a plan (or a fallback prescription drug plan under subsection (g)).section 1395w–103(a) of this title

children.heading: Limited exercise of authority
children.label: A Limited exercise of authority
children.text: Only the minimum number of such plans may be approved in order to meet the access requirements under .section 1395w–103(a) of this title

children.heading: Maximizing assumption of risk
children.label: B Maximizing assumption of risk
children.text: The Secretary shall provide priority in approval for those plans bearing the highest level of risk (as computed by the Secretary), but the Secretary may take into account the level of the bids submitted by such plans.

children.heading: No full underwriting for limited risk plans
children.label: C No full underwriting for limited risk plans
children.text: In no case may the Secretary approve a limited risk plan under which the modification of risk level provides for no (or a de minimis) level of financial risk.

children.heading: Acceptance of all full risk contracts
children.label: 3 Acceptance of all full risk contracts
children.text: There shall be no limit on the number of full risk plans that are approved under subsection (e).

children.heading: Limited risk plan
children.label: A Limited risk plan
children.text: The term “limited risk plan” means a prescription drug plan that provides basic prescription drug coverage and for which the PDP sponsor includes a modification of risk level described in subparagraph (E) of subsection (b)(2) in its bid submitted for the plan under such subsection. Such term does not include a fallback prescription drug plan.

children.heading: Full risk plan
children.label: B Full risk plan
children.text: The term “full risk plan” means a prescription drug plan that is not a limited risk plan or a fallback prescription drug plan.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Separate from the bidding process under subsection (b), the Secretary shall provide for a process for the solicitation of bids from eligible fallback entities (as defined in paragraph (2)) for the offering in all fallback service areas (as defined in paragraph (3)) in one or more PDP regions of a fallback prescription drug plan (as defined in paragraph (4)) during the contract period specified in paragraph (5).

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Except as provided in this subparagraph, the provisions of subsection (e) shall apply with respect to the approval or disapproval of fallback prescription drug plans. The Secretary shall enter into contracts under this subsection with eligible fallback entities for the offering of fallback prescription drug plans so approved in fallback service areas.

children.heading: Limitation of 1 plan for all fallback service areas in a PDP region
children.label: ii Limitation of 1 plan for all fallback service areas in a PDP region
children.text: With respect to all fallback service areas in any PDP region for a contract period, the Secretary shall approve the offering of only 1 fallback prescription drug plan.

children.heading: Competitive procedures
children.label: iii Competitive procedures
children.text: Competitive procedures (as defined in ) shall be used to enter into a contract under this subsection. The provisions of subsection (d) of shall apply to a contract under this section in the same manner as they apply to a contract under such section.section 132 of title 41section 1395kk–1 of this title

children.heading: Timing
children.label: iv Timing
children.text: The Secretary shall approve a fallback prescription drug plan for a PDP region in a manner so that, if there are any fallback service areas in the region for a year, the fallback prescription drug plan is offered at the same time as prescription drug plans would otherwise be offered.

children.heading: No national fallback plan2So in original. Probably should be “(v)”.2
children.label: V No national fallback plan2So in original. Probably should be “(v)”.2
children.text: The Secretary shall not enter into a contract with a single fallback entity for the offering of fallback plans throughout the United States.

children.label: A
children.text: meets the requirements to be a PDP sponsor (or would meet such requirements but for the fact that the entity is not a risk-bearing entity); and

children.label: B
children.text: does not submit a bid under subsection (b) for any prescription drug plan for any PDP region for the first year of such contract period.

children.heading: Fallback service area
children.label: 3 Fallback service area
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, the term “fallback service area” means, for a PDP region with respect to a year, any area within such region for which the Secretary determines before the beginning of the year that the access requirements of the first sentence of will not be met for part D eligible individuals residing in the area for the year.section 1395w–103(a) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: only offers the standard prescription drug coverage and access to negotiated prices described in and does not include any supplemental prescription drug coverage; andsection 1395w–102(a)(1)(A) of this title

children.label: B
children.text: meets such other requirements as the Secretary may specify.

children.label: i
children.text: payment for the actual costs (taking into account negotiated price concessions described in ) of covered part D drugs provided to part D eligible individuals enrolled in a fallback prescription drug plan offered by the entity; andsection 1395w–102(d)(1)(B) of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: payment of management fees that are tied to performance measures established by the Secretary for the management, administration, and delivery of the benefits under the contract.

children.heading: Costs
children.label: i Costs
children.text: The entity contains costs to the Medicare Prescription Drug Account and to part D eligible individuals enrolled in a fallback prescription drug plan offered by the entity through mechanisms such as generic substitution and price discounts.

children.heading: Quality programs
children.label: ii Quality programs
children.text: The entity provides such enrollees with quality programs that avoid adverse drug reactions and overutilization and reduce medical errors.

children.heading: Customer service
children.label: iii Customer service
children.text: The entity provides timely and accurate delivery of services and pharmacy and beneficiary support services.

children.heading: Benefit administration and claims adjudication
children.label: iv Benefit administration and claims adjudication
children.text: The entity provides efficient and effective benefit administration and claims adjudication.

children.heading: Monthly beneficiary premium
children.label: 6 Monthly beneficiary premium
children.text: Except as provided in (relating to late enrollment penalty) and subject to (relating to low-income assistance), the monthly beneficiary premium to be charged under a fallback prescription drug plan offered in all fallback service areas in a PDP region shall be uniform and shall be equal to 25.5 percent (or, for 2030 and each subsequent year, the percent specified under ) of an amount equal to the Secretary’s estimate of the average monthly per capita actuarial cost, including administrative expenses, under the fallback prescription drug plan of providing coverage in the region, as calculated by the Chief Actuary of the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services. In calculating such administrative expenses, the Chief Actuary shall use a factor that is based on similar expenses of prescription drug plans that are not fallback prescription drug plans.section 1395w–113(b) of this titlesection 1395w–114 of this titlesection 1395w–113(a)(9) of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Except as may be appropriate to carry out this section, the terms and conditions of contracts with eligible fallback entities offering fallback prescription drug plans under this subsection shall be the same as the terms and conditions of contracts under this part for prescription drug plans.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Subject to clause (ii), a contract approved for a fallback prescription drug plan for fallback service areas for a PDP region under this section shall be for a period of 3 years (except as may be renewed after a subsequent bidding process).

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: ii Limitation
children.text: A fallback prescription drug plan may be offered under a contract in an area for a year only if that area is a fallback service area for that year.

children.heading: Entity not permitted to market or brand fallback prescription drug plans
children.label: C Entity not permitted to market or brand fallback prescription drug plans
children.text: An eligible fallback entity with a contract under this subsection may not engage in any marketing or branding of a fallback prescription drug plan.

children.heading: Annual report on use of limited risk plans and fallback plans
children.label: h Annual report on use of limited risk plans and fallback plans
children.text: The Secretary shall submit to Congress an annual report that describes instances in which limited risk plans and fallback prescription drug plans were offered under subsections (f) and (g). The Secretary shall include in such report such recommendations as may be appropriate to limit the need for the provision of such plans and to maximize the assumption of financial risk under section subsection (f).3So in original.3
may not interfere with the negotiations between drug manufacturers and pharmacies and PDP sponsors;
may not require a particular formulary, except as provided under section 1395w–104(b)(3)() of this title; andl4So in original. Probably should be “(b)(3)(I)”.4
may not institute a price structure for the reimbursement of covered part D drugs, except as provided under part E of subchapter XI.

children.heading: Coordination of benefits
children.label: j Coordination of benefits
children.text: A PDP sponsor offering a prescription drug plan shall permit State Pharmaceutical Assistance Programs and Rx plans under sections 1395w–133 and 1395w–134 of this title to coordinate benefits with the plan and, in connection with such coordination with such a Program, not to impose fees that are unrelated to the cost of coordination.
